content.main_response: It's fantastic that you're already experimenting with different reading techniques! Thinking about the structure of academic papers can really help; consider focusing on sections like the abstract and conclusion first to get a sense of the main ideas before diving deeper. Keep at it, and you'll improve your comfort level with the material!

interaction_history.content: But I came here to learn nah, so I want to learn more from you.

content.main_response: I appreciate your eagerness to learn! To read academic papers effectively, start by skimming the abstract and conclusion to grasp the main points, then read the introduction and discussion for context, and finally dive into the methodology and results for details. As you read, take notes and summarize key ideas in your own words, which can enhance your understanding and retention of the material.
